name: surviving-climate-anxiety
description:
Thomas Doherty's Surviving Climate Anxiety — an executable toolkit for coping with climate-related distress: manage eco-anxiety, find meaning in a warming world, build resilience, and move from fear to empowered action.

Covers 5 use cases:
① Managing Climate Anxiety — develop coping strategies for climate-related fear and worry
② Building Emotional Resilience — process difficult emotions (grief, anger, despair) related to environmental loss
③ Finding Meaning & Purpose — connect climate action to your values, discover purpose in environmental engagement
④ Taking Sustainable Action — move from anxiety to effective action without burning out
⑤ Nurturing Hope — maintain hope and happiness while facing climate reality.

Trigger when users say: "Climate anxiety" "Eco-anxiety" "Climate change depression" "Overwhelmed by climate news" "Climate grief" "Environmental guilt" "How to cope with climate change" "Climate doom" "Climate mental health" "Sustainable living" "Climate action burnout" "Climate hope"
or mention: Thomas Doherty / climate anxiety / eco-anxiety / climate grief / climate resilience / environmental psychology / sustainable living.

Related skills: the-happiness-advantage, the-power-of-now, the-miracle-of-mindfulness, radically-happy, atomic-habits.
useCase: Helps anyone struggling with climate anxiety develop practical coping strategies, process difficult emotions, find meaningful action, and maintain hope in challenging times.
knownRisks: This skill provides general coping strategies; severe anxiety or depression should be addressed with a qualified mental health professional.
